overview.text: Marine Rescue NSW is the State's primary volunteer marine search-and-rescue service. It coordinates on-water rescue, vessel safety monitoring (logged Trip Watch), missing-vessel response and emergency communications across the NSW coast and major inland waterways.

items: Established in 2009 by merging the Australian Volunteer Coast Guard, Royal Volunteer Coastal Patrol and Volunteer Rescue Association marine units.
Around 3,000 volunteers across 46 units from Point Danger to Eden, plus Alpine Lakes and Moama on the Murray.
Operates a fleet of more than 100 rescue vessels and a 24/7 State Communications Centre.
Tasked through Marine Area Command (NSW Police) for life-at-risk incidents.
